Title: MiR319-mediated ethylene biosynthesis, signalling and salt stress response in switchgrass

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM or 300 mM NaCl; ACC; ethylene; AgNO3
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supportive target for stacking with miR319/PvPCF5 to enhance K+ uptake and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvHAK5 expression is induced by low ACC and reduced by AgNO3. 2. It is implicated downstream of ethylene signaling in improved K+ uptake under salt stress. 3. It is not the primary focus gene but part of the validated output pathway.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by salt stress and ethylene/low ACC
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to K+/Na+ homeostasis during salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced K+ uptake and salt tolerance downstream of ethylene

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM or 300 mM NaCl; ACC; AgNO3
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary target for improving ROS scavenging in salt-tolerant engineering stacks
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvPOD53 is induced by low ACC and reduced when ethylene signaling is inhibited by AgNO3. 2. It is associated with lower H2O2 accumulation in salt-treated seedlings. 3. It acts downstream of ethylene signaling rather than as the main focus gene.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging to reduce H2O2 under salt stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; DAB/H2O2 assays in treatment comparisons
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with reduced ROS accumulation and improved salt tolerance downstream of ethylene

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complementary target for stacking with ethylene-related regulators to improve Na+ sequestration/exclusion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvNHX was examined as a salt-response gene under ACC treatments. 2. Its expression did not show the same significant ACC response as several ethylene-signaling outputs. 3. It represents an ethylene-independent or partially connected salt-tolerance component.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured under ACC/AgNO3 treatments; response less pronounced than other tested genes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Na+ homeostasis under salt stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with salt tolerance but not established as main causal regulator of ethylene output in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 300 mM NaCl salt stress; exogenous ethylene/ACC treatments in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher ethylene can be beneficial only within a dose window; excessive ET/ACC reduced salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of ACO genes is associated with increased ethylene production and improved salt tolerance in OE-miR319 and PvPCF5-SRDX plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ACC to ethylene in the terminal step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to moderately elevate ethylene production for salt tolerance, but dosage must be fine-tuned to avoid excess-ethylene penalties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two tested ACO genes were induced early by salt stress. 2. ACO transcripts were upregulated in OE-miR319 and PvPCF5-SRDX plants. 3. Increased ACO expression correlated with higher ethylene production and better salt tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to two tested switchgrass ACO genes and three ACO1-encoding DEGs from RNA-seq, but specific locus IDs are not provided in the excerpt
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates salt stress tolerance; modulates ROS scavenging and ion homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced at 0.5 h after 300 mM NaCl; higher in OE-miR319 and PvPCF5-SRDX lines
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Yang cycle to ethylene output branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; ethylene production assay; transgenic comparison in OE-miR319, MIM319, and PvPCF5-SRDX lines

Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Ethylene induces miR319 expression, indicating positive feedback/crosstalk between ethylene and miR319
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene biosynthesis and downstream salt-response signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of Osa-MIR319b; target mimicry MIM319; qRT-PCR; ethylene measurement; salt tolerance assays; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 300 mM NaCl; exogenous ethylene; ACC; AgNO3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes salt tolerance by improving ROS management and K+/Na+ homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory engineering target to improve biomass and salt tolerance via tuning ethylene biosynthesis, but should avoid over-accumulation of ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The overexpression construct used rice Osa-MIR319b in switchgrass; endogenous switchgrass miR319 was also salt- and ethylene-responsive
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess ethylene is detrimental; miR319 also changes leaf morphology and biomass
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates ethylene synthesis and enhances salt tolerance in switchgrass
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced rapidly by 300 mM NaCl and by exogenous ethylene in seedlings/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR319 is induced by salt stress and exogenous ethylene. 2. Overexpression increases ethylene production, biomass, K+ retention and salt tolerance, whereas MIM319 has opposite effects. 3. miR319 represses TCP targets and shifts transcriptome toward higher ACO and lower Met-cycle gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central upstream regulator that fine-tunes ethylene biosynthesis and thereby determines salt tolerance outcome
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TCP/PCF targets; indirectly promotes ACO expression and represses methionine salvage genes

Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of miR319-ethylene crosstalk module; downregulated by salt stress and ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for PvPCF5-SRDX repression construct
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5' RLM-RACE validating miR319 cleavage; dominant repression with PvPCF5-SRDX; qRT-PCR; ethylene production assay; salt tolerance phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ethylene promotes salt tolerance, ROS control, and ion homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; acts as negative regulator of ethylene biosynthesis pathway
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Repression or editing of PvPCF5 is a promising strategy to improve biomass and salt tolerance through elevated endogenous ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PvPCF5 transcript is cleaved by miR319 between bases 10 and 11 of the target site; repression by SRDX phenocopies loss of function
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered plant architecture with wider leaves and taller plants; excess ethylene risk if over-manipulated
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Methionine salvage cycle genes (e.g., SAMD1, SRM, MTI1, DEP1, ARD) and indirectly/negatively ACO genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates ethylene synthesis and salt tolerance; repression of PvPCF5 increases ethylene and enhances salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by exogenous ethylene and early salt stress; reduced in OE-miR319 and increased in MIM319 plants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvPCF5 is a validated direct target of miR319 by RLM-RACE. 2. Repression of PvPCF5 doubles ethylene production and improves salt tolerance. 3. PvPCF5 repression lowers Na+ accumulation, raises K+/Na+ ratio, and downregulates Met-cycle genes while upregulating ACO genes.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context for downstream pathway changes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator within the miR319-PvPCF5 module controlling endogenous ethylene level under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively associated with ACO expression and positively associated with methionine salvage gene expression
